issues: outline_weight: amulet cord/chain uses thick white pixels that read heavier and brighter than the 1px dark outlines on all peers
outline_color: dominant white outline on cord is absent from every peer; peers use dark-gray/black outlines exclusively
highlight_intensity: gem facet highlight (near-white pixel) is far more saturated and prominent than the subtle single-tone highlights on peer helmets

color_temperature: peers are overwhelmingly cool-gray metallic with at most one warm accent; amulet introduces a vivid mid-green gem + stark white cord that shifts temperature visibly outside peer range
palette_overlap: white is essentially unused across the entire peer sheet; its dominance on the cord is a palette discontinuity
shadow_count: gem renders 3+ distinct shading tones; peers max out at 2 tones per sprite
detail_density: gem facets add detail layers not present in peer sprites at equivalent scale; peers are intentionally flat
charm_level: peers are utilitarian, muted, low-affect war-gear; amulet gem sparkle reads as noticeably 'cuter' / more decorative, mismatching the dour peer register
reasoning: Ignoring item-type difference (amulet vs helmet is fine), the candidate still breaks consistency on at least 6 of 11 dims. The white cord is the single biggest flag — no peer uses white as a primary structural color. The gem's multi-tone facet shading and bright highlight push detail density and highlight intensity above the peer ceiling. Palette overlap is weak: peer palette is restricted to grays, blacks, and desaturated accent metals; vibrant green + white is a new color family. Shadow count and detail density are both elevated. Charm level mismatch is real — the sparkling gem reads decorative against uniformly grim helmet silhouettes. Score 4 / fail.
